proposed_from.topic: Sharp MSM partial identification of the CVaR / rank-dependent welfare of a policy under unobserved confounding. Extend Schroder-Frauen-Feuerriegel (2025, arXiv:2502.13022) confounding-robust off-policy bounds from the MEAN welfare V(pi)=E[sum_a pi(a|X) Y(a)] to the lower-tail welfare W_tau(pi)=CVaR_tau(Y(pi)) (Y lower=better; the policy minimizes worst-case tail risk). Central proposition: the worst-case MSM tilt does NOT commute with the CVaR quantile, so the sharp lower endpoint is the nested-quantile object CVaR_lower_tau(pi)=inf_eta{ eta + (1/tau) E_X[ worst-case-tilted E[(Y-eta)+ | X, pi] ] } with the inner tilt at the Dorn-Frauen level alpha=Gamma/(1+Gamma); prove it is STRICTLY tighter than CVaR applied to the pointwise average-potential-outcome band (the non-commutation gap is the named scalar). Computation: 1-D convex line search in eta, each step a closed-form tilted truncated mean. Estimation rung: efficient influence function for CVaR_lower_tau(pi) (nuisances eta(P), per-cell MSM quantile cutoffs, truncated outcome regressions), cross-fit one-step estimator with asymptotic normality and an Imbens-Manski interval over the partially-identified CVaR.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue proposal is pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1, which concerns coordinatewise QTE envelopes and finite rank certificates under marginal sensitivity. This proposal targets the process-level CVaR endpoint for a fixed policy, the scalar non-commutation gap against coordinatewise-band CVaR, and the eta-indexed one-step endpoint score. pid_bound_intersection_inference_v1 supplies only generic interval-intersection inference context, and pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 supplies rectangular-relaxation diagnostics rather than MSM-CVaR partial identification.","published_axis":"HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2025SharpOPE and JinXuZhengZhou2025RobustEfficientPolicy are the closest MSM robust policy-learning comparators, but their policy welfare objects are mean or worst-case mean targets. FanQiXu2025AlphaExpectedWelfare studies alpha-tail welfare without MSM partial identification. DornGuo2021QuantileBalancing and MastenPoirierRen2025RelaxingUnconfoundedness cover pointwise sensitivity or distributional bounds, not the joint rank-dependent CVaR operator. DornGuoKallus2021DoublySharp gives the closest orthogonal MSM endpoint score, but not the generated truncated-loss CVaR endpoint score."}
seed_list: Introduce the sharp nested MSM-CVaR endpoint for a fixed stochastic policy and prove a cross-fitted one-step estimator for the endpoint.
Prove a strict non-commutation theorem: CVaR of pointwise MSM bands is looser than the nested eta-indexed MSM-CVaR endpoint, with an equality-case common-tilt characterization.
Derive the efficient influence function for the sharp MSM-CVaR endpoint under a unique robust quantile and convert endpoint CLTs into an Imbens-Manski interval.
Extend alpha-expected welfare policy learning to hidden-confounding MSM partial identification by minimizing the estimated worst-case CVaR endpoint over a policy class.
Characterize when stochastic multi-action MSM-CVaR admits independent actionwise tilts and when cross-action latent compatibility creates a strict coupled endpoint.
Build a process-level support-function representation for MSM rank-dependent welfare that cannot be recovered from coordinatewise QTE or DTE envelopes.
Replace minimax IPW robust policy improvement with a one-dimensional nested-CVaR line search and prove a fixed-policy robust improvement theorem.
Prove an interchange theorem for Rockafellar-Uryasev CVaR and MSM adversarial tilting, including necessary and sufficient conditions for minimax equality.
Construct a calibrated endpoint covariance rule for partially identified MSM-CVaR intervals that is not generic active-set max inference.
Upgrade the failed QTE marginal-sensitivity envelope into a simultaneous eta-process theorem for tilted truncated losses.
Give a finite two-action three-reward witness where rectangular actionwise CVaR bands are strictly looser than one latent-compatible MSM-CVaR law.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce the sharp nested MSM-CVaR endpoint for a fixed stochastic policy and prove a cross-fitted one-step estimator for the endpoint.

anchor_paper.author: Hess et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp efficient MSM mean off-policy value bounds leave tail/rank welfare unresolved.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper frames sharp mean value bounds and efficient policy learning; the natural unresolved follow-up is replacing the linear value by CVaR.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends mean to CVaR; stronger because the eta optimizer changes the least-favorable tilt and creates a nested endpoint.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2025SharpOPE by applying their mean theorem to (Y-eta)+.
seed_details.rebuttal: The fixed-eta step is immediate, but sharpness after the outer eta minimization and the non-commutation gap require a process-level law argument.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a strict non-commutation theorem: CVaR of pointwise MSM bands is looser than the nested eta-indexed MSM-CVaR endpoint, with an equality-case common-tilt characterization.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn and Guo
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp MSM sensitivity requires solving the direct optimization, not indirect wide intervals.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their direct-sharpness message suggests checking whether a pointwise envelope remains sharp after a rank-dependent operator chooses eta.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample risk; stronger because the target is a named coordinatewise-band workflow and the witness is nondegenerate finite support.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Jensen or minimax inequality applied to known distributional bands.
seed_details.rebuttal: The contribution is the strict positive gap and equality condition for MSM least-favorable tilts, not the weak inequality alone.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the efficient influence function for the sharp MSM-CVaR endpoint under a unique robust quantile and convert endpoint CLTs into an Imbens-Manski interval.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Doubly-sharp estimators achieve semiparametric efficiency for sharp MSM ATE bounds.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their EIF construction for mean endpoints naturally asks what changes when the target has eta and a kinked positive part.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator extension; stronger because the generated truncated-loss score has a quantile envelope term absent from mean endpoints.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard CVaR delta method plus the Dorn-Guo-Kallus score.
seed_details.rebuttal: The score must combine MSM quantile balancing, the CVaR envelope theorem, and partial-ID endpoint covariance, which is not in the comparator.

seed_details.one_line: Extend alpha-expected welfare policy learning to hidden-confounding MSM partial identification by minimizing the estimated worst-case CVaR endpoint over a policy class.

anchor_paper.author: Fan et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Alpha-expected welfare optimizes worst-off-alpha policy welfare under conventional identification.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper's tail-welfare policy objective invites the same objective under hidden confounding rather than conventional identification.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Policy-learning extension; stronger if paired with uniform regret and a sharp endpoint rather than fixed-policy evaluation only.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows by plugging the nested endpoint into existing policy-learning regret bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The eta search and MSM adversary create nonregular nuisance classes, so the uniform score and regret proof are new.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize when stochastic multi-action MSM-CVaR admits independent actionwise tilts and when cross-action latent compatibility creates a strict coupled endpoint.

anchor_paper.author: Frauen et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Generalized sensitivity bounds cover multiple treatment settings and distributional effects but not randomized-policy CVaR.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their generalized multi-treatment setup raises whether randomized policy tail events can be optimized actionwise or require a coupled latent law.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation; stronger because cross-action compatibility can strictly tighten a rectangular endpoint.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a standard finite latent-type sharpness program with extra action cells.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nonlinearity is rank-based across policy mixtures, so actionwise sharpness need not paste into a policy-CVaR law.

seed_details.one_line: Build a process-level support-function representation for MSM rank-dependent welfare that cannot be recovered from coordinatewise QTE or DTE envelopes.

anchor_paper.author: Masten et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp QTE and DTE bounds under relaxations including MSM do not settle nonlinear policy risk functionals.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their distributional bounds suggest asking which nonlinear welfare functionals require a common process rather than thresholdwise envelopes.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Coordinatewise envelope warning; stronger because it names an explicit rank-dependent support functional.
seed_details.referee_objection: CVaR is a functional of the CDF, so sharp DTE/QTE bounds should already imply it.
seed_details.rebuttal: Separate sharp CDF values may use incompatible least-favorable laws; CVaR needs one law across eta.

seed_details.one_line: Replace minimax IPW robust policy improvement with a one-dimensional nested-CVaR line search and prove a fixed-policy robust improvement theorem.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Confounding-robust policy improvement minimizes worst-case mean regret over propensity-weight uncertainty.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Their minimax robust improvement framework asks whether a sharp closed-form tail-risk endpoint can avoid unstable IPW minimax optimization.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends robust mean regret to tail risk; stronger only with a named line-search endpoint and improvement guarantee.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a computational restatement of Kallus-Zhou with CVaR losses.
seed_details.rebuttal: The policy risk is not an expectation of a fixed loss until eta is solved jointly with the MSM adversary.

seed_details.one_line: Prove an interchange theorem for Rockafellar-Uryasev CVaR and MSM adversarial tilting, including necessary and sufficient conditions for minimax equality.

anchor_paper.author: Rockafellar and Uryasev
anchor_paper.claim: CVaR can be optimized through a one-dimensional eta minimization of an expected positive part.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The variational formula asks when the outer eta minimization can be interchanged with an MSM adversarial expectation.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Iff theorem; stronger if both directions identify common-tilt equality, weaker if only sufficient.
seed_details.referee_objection: Sion minimax gives the interchange immediately under convexity.
seed_details.rebuttal: The feasible MSM law class is tied to observed conditional laws and sharp quantile-balancing weights, so common-law sharpness is the issue.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a calibrated endpoint covariance rule for partially identified MSM-CVaR intervals that is not generic active-set max inference.

anchor_paper.author: Imbens and Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Confidence intervals for interval-identified scalar parameters require accounting for sampling error and identification width.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Once MSM-CVaR is interval-identified, endpoint CLTs must be turned into scalar coverage without collapsing to generic intersection inference.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Inference calibration; weaker unless tied to a new CVaR endpoint score, so best used as a rung.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Imbens-Manski applied after estimating two endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: As standalone yes; as part of the CVaR endpoint score it supplies coverage but not the novelty kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Upgrade the failed QTE marginal-sensitivity envelope into a simultaneous eta-process theorem for tilted truncated losses.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Prior failed run was pushed beyond coordinatewise envelopes toward process-level inference or identification.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Upgrade Conjecture 2...to a concrete simultaneous-inference or LP-complexity theorem

seed_details.anchor_followup: n/a -- prior-proposal anchor; the reviewer requested a process-level theorem rather than another envelope summary.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE/QTE envelope variant; stronger because eta-process truncated losses expose compatibility across thresholds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats the failed QTE marginal-sensitivity proposal with a different functional label.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposal changes the object from QTE envelopes to CVaR's eta-indexed support functional and adds a sharp endpoint theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite two-action three-reward witness where rectangular actionwise CVaR bands are strictly looser than one latent-compatible MSM-CVaR law.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Prior downgraded run warned that rectangular diagnostics need a named target and numeric strict-gap witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: give a fully numeric two-action, three-reward witness

seed_details.anchor_followup: n/a -- prior-proposal anchor; the reviewer asked for a numeric strict-gap witness for a non-rectangular policy bound.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample; stronger if the refuted workflow is coordinatewise-band CVaR and the witness is nondegenerate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is another generic rectangular-relaxation no-go without a published target.
seed_details.rebuttal: The named target is CVaR applied to pointwise MSM mean/CDF bands, anchored in the current MSM and QTE literature.

proposed_from.literature_map:
HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2025SharpOPE derives sharp semiparametrically efficient MSM bounds for mean off-policy value, not CVaR tail welfare.
DornGuo2021QuantileBalancing shows indirect MSM sensitivity intervals can be conservative, motivating direct sharp optimization rather than coordinatewise envelopes.
DornGuoKallus2021DoublySharp gives doubly-sharp orthogonal scores for sharp MSM ATE endpoints, the closest estimator template.
FanQiXu2025AlphaExpectedWelfare develops policy learning for worst-off-alpha welfare under conventional identification, without hidden-confounding partial identification.
RockafellarUryasev2000CVaR supplies the one-dimensional eta variational formula for CVaR.
MastenPoirierRen2025RelaxingUnconfoundedness derives sharp QTE/DTE bounds under relaxation classes including MSM, but nonlinear policy risk functionals remain unresolved.
FrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2023GeneralizedCSA handles generalized sensitivity bounds and multiple treatments but not randomized-policy CVaR.
KallusZhou2018ConfoundingRobustPolicy motivates robust policy improvement under unobserved confounding using mean regret.
ImbensManski2004PartialIDCI is the interval-identified scalar CI template once lower and upper CVaR endpoints have CLTs.
AtheyWager2021PolicyLearning supplies the observational policy-learning regret grammar that would consume a uniform version of the fixed-policy endpoint.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjectures 1-3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The endpoint equality, non-commutation gap, and one-step limit law are intentionally unproved Stage -1 conjectures.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Advance only as conjectural Stage 0 solve targets; acceptance here is not a proof of the claims.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The smooth one-step CLT is restricted to the unique robust quantile and positive-density regime; mass points are excluded.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Inference claim is bound to the smooth unique-optimizer regime in Assumptions 4-6.
